summary:
The following are the key takeaways from Roku's earnings conference call:
- Platform revenue grew 17% YoY in Q3, or 19% excluding political and friendly revenue, with Q4 guidance implying approximately 20% growth on the same basis
- Achieved positive operating income in Q3 for the first time since fiscal 2021, with Q4 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $145M being the highest ever
- Full year EBITDA margins expected to improve 200 basis points YoY to approximately 8.4%, with similar improvement expected in 2026
- Trailing 12-month free cash flow exceeded $440M, with the company remaining CapEx light and growing free cash flow faster than EBITDA
- Completed $500M share repurchase with Q3 total dilution of 130 basis points, the lowest dilution in any quarter in company history
- Approximately 90% of advertisers on Ads Manager self-serve platform in Q3 were new to Roku, indicating strong expansion into performance marketing
- Amazon DSP integration went live in early Q4 with strong customer interest, following the successful Trade Desk integration pattern from 2024
- Premium subscriptions business is growing exceptionally well with new Tier 1 service launches in Q3 and more planned for 2026
- New home screen redesign is testing well with positive feedback, showing improved viewer satisfaction, engagement, and monetization, expected to roll out in 2026
- Management expressed confidence in reaching 100 million streaming households in 2026 with ARPU growing faster than household growth due to platform revenue initiatives
